tool_args.label: 34th Street Select Bus Service Project Analysis Report

payload.title: 34th Street Select Bus Service Project Analysis Report
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation
payload.description: Project Analysis Report for the 34th Street Select Bus Service project prepared by NYCDOT in collaboration with MTA New York City Transit and MTA Bus Company

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: 34th Street Select Bus Service
tool_args.raw_text: The New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT), in collaboration with the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) New York City Transit (NYCT) and in cooperation with MTA Bus Company (MTA Bus), is proposing the 34th Street Select Bus Service (SBS) project (the "Proposed Project") to implement enhanced bus service along 34th Street from the Hudson River to the East River in Manhattan.

payload.description: Enhanced bus service project implementing offset and curbside bus lanes, SBS stations, bulb outs, transit signal priority, and loading zones along 34th Street from the Hudson River to the East River in Manhattan.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT)
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: MTA New York City Transit (NYCT)
tool_args.raw_text: Metropolitan Transportation Authority New York City Transit Bus Company

warnings: entity.parent_entity is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Parent entities are relation context. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company
tool_args.raw_text: Metropolitan Transportation Authority New York City Transit Bus Company

payload.description: Operates between Twelfth Avenue and the East 34th Street Ferry Terminal along 34th Street. Eastbound from Javits Center to 34th Street Ferry Terminal; westbound via Eleventh Avenue, 33rd Street, Twelfth Avenue to Javits Center.

Operates between Waterside Plaza (FDR Drive) and Port Authority Bus Terminal. Uses 34th Street between Eighth/Ninth Avenue and FDR Drive Service Road/Second Avenue. Formerly the M16 bus route, renamed November 13, 2011.

payload.limits: From Hudson River to East River (FDR Drive to Twelfth Avenue)
payload.description: Approximately 2-mile long crosstown corridor in Midtown Manhattan with roadbed width varying from 52 to 60 feet.
Construction of the Proposed Project would commence in the summer of 2012
Most construction work would be completed by late fall of 2012

tool_args.label: Segment III (Tenth to Twelfth Avenues) Completion - Late 2013

payload.description: Construction of project elements between Tenth and Twelfth Avenues (Route 9A) delayed to approximately late 2013 to avoid conflicts with No. 7 Train Extension and Hudson Yards projects
Offset bus lanes to improve bus speeds. About two-thirds (63%) of exclusive bus-lane-miles on the corridor would be offset from the curb.
payload.location_text: 34th Street from First Avenue to Eleventh Avenue

normalized_location.raw_text: 34th Street from First Avenue to Eleventh Avenue

payload.description: Curbside loading and unloading zones on every block of the corridor, providing about 240 curbside loading spaces during morning and early evening periods.
payload.location_text: Every block of 34th Street from First Avenue to Twelfth Avenue (Route 9A)

normalized_location.raw_text: Every block of 34th Street from First Avenue to Twelfth Avenue (Route 9A)

payload.description: 24 new SBS stations (12 eastbound, 12 westbound) with new shelters, benches, signage, and passenger information. Stations would be 140 feet long to accommodate two 60-foot buses.
14 sidewalk extensions (bulb outs) at 18 SBS stations providing about 20,400 square feet of new pedestrian space. Bulb outs extend 8 to 9 feet into the roadway.
payload.location_text: At SBS station locations along 34th Street

normalized_location.raw_text: At SBS station locations along 34th Street

payload.description: 10 right turn bays at key intersections to reduce congestion, plus 1 left turn bay at First Avenue and East 34th Street.

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority Equipment and Signal Timing Adjustments

payload.description: Transit signal priority equipment and adjustments to signal timings at certain intersections along 34th Street and adjacent streets (33rd, 34th, and 35th Streets).
Seven express bus stops (two westbound, five eastbound) for exclusive use by express buses with passenger amenities including shelters and information signage.
payload.location_text: Westbound: between Broadway and Seventh Avenue, and between Eighth and Ninth Avenues. Eastbound: between Seventh Avenue and Eighth Avenue, Broadway and Seventh Avenue, Madison Avenue and Fifth Avenue, Lexington Avenue and Park Avenue, and Third Avenue and Lexington Avenue.

normalized_location.raw_text: Westbound: between Broadway and Seventh Avenue, and between Eighth and Ninth Avenues. Eastbound: between Seventh Avenue and Eighth Avenue, Broadway and Seventh Avenue, Madison Avenue and Fifth Avenue, Lexington Avenue and Park Avenue, and Third Avenue and Lexington Avenue.

payload.context: After 2008 improvements (curbside bus lanes, signal adjustments, fleet replacement)

payload.context: Round trip distance of M34 bus on 34th Street

tool_args.label: Bus Speed Improvement 17% from 2008 Curbside Lanes

payload.raw_value_text: improved crosstown bus speeds on the corridor by 17 percent
payload.context: Improvement after NYCDOT installed curbside bus lanes along 34th Street in 2008

tool_args.label: Expected 20% Travel Time Decrease from SBS

payload.raw_value_text: average travel time has decreased by 20 percent
payload.context: Examples from Bx12 and M15 Select Bus Service indicate expected similar level for 34th Street SBS
Examples from similar SBS projects (Bx12, M15) indicate expected similar level

payload.claim_text: Overall, the analyses conclude that the Proposed Project would not result in any significant adverse impacts
payload.subject: 34th Street Select Bus Service Proposed Project

payload.claim_text: Environmental benefits from enhanced access to transit and regional air quality through a reduction in vehicle miles traveled.
payload.subject: 34th Street SBS

payload.claim_text: Construction of the Proposed Project would last less than 12 months.
payload.subject: 34th Street SBS construction

tool_args.label: Pedestrian Benefits - Bulb Outs and Crosswalk Standardization

payload.claim_text: At the majority of Select Bus Service station locations, expanded pedestrian circulation space. Introduction of bulb outs at some corners along 34th Street, reducing conflict time between pedestrians and vehicular traffic. Crosswalk widths would be standardized to 15 feet along the 34th Street corridor.
payload.subject: 34th Street SBS pedestrian improvements

payload.claim_text: Reduction in bus trip time, especially idling time at bus stops/stations, resulting in fuel savings and greenhouse gas emissions reductions.
payload.subject: 34th Street SBS

tool_args.label: 34th Street SBS Project serves M34 Route

payload.description: The new bus lanes and stations would be served by the M34 Select Bus Service

tool_args.label: 34th Street SBS Project serves M34A Route

payload.description: The new bus lanes and stations would be served by the M34A Select Bus Service
The Proposed Project would be constructed within 34th Street from FDR Drive to Twelfth Avenue
M34 travels the length of 34th Street operating between the Javits Center and the FDR Drive
M34A travels on 34th Street between Eighth/Ninth Avenue and FDR Drive Service Road/Second Avenue

payload.description: The 34th Street SBS project includes offset bus lanes

tool_args.label: SBS Project includes curbside loading zones

payload.description: The 34th Street SBS project includes curbside loading and unloading zones
The 34th Street SBS project includes 24 new SBS stations
The 34th Street SBS project includes 14 sidewalk extensions (bulb outs)
The 34th Street SBS project includes 10 right turn bays and 1 left turn bay

payload.description: The 34th Street SBS project includes transit signal priority equipment and signal timing adjustments
Construction would commence in summer 2012

tool_args.label: 34 Additional Bus Trips Needed in AM Peak Hour

payload.raw_value_text: 34 additional bus trips in the AM peak hour
payload.context: Without project changes, to accommodate future development demand

tool_args.label: 31 Additional Bus Trips Needed in PM Peak Hour

payload.raw_value_text: 31 additional bus trips in the PM peak hour
payload.context: Without project changes, to accommodate future development demand

tool_args.label: M34: 60% of Travel Time in Stopped Time Before Off-Board Fare

payload.raw_value_text: Only 40 percent of the M34's total travel time was spent in motion. Of the 60 percent in stopped time, approximately 22 percent was for waiting at traffic signals, and 38 percent was to board and alight passengers (dwell time).
payload.context: Prior to November 2011 off-board fare collection implementation
